{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks about the lateral spread of dental caries. From the provided documents, there is no direct mention of enamel spindles, enamel lamellae, or striae of Retzius in relation to the lateral spread of caries. However, the documents discuss the dentinoenamel junction as the area where caries can spread laterally. Specifically, Document [0] states 'When viewed radiographically, interproximal caries begin apical to the contact point and are cone-shaped.
